vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of severe degradation. The registry-expected specimens (P1-P4) are not visually identifiable as living plants. The environment is dominated by soil, debris, and structural hardware rather than botanical life.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No String of Nickels visible.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No Mexican Mint visible.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss: No Pothos visible; White Rabbit anchor is absent.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No Silver Guest visible.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, damp, and potentially anaerobic. No signs of active root systems.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Presence of a metal spoon (T-3), a white cylindrical object (T-1, T-4), and various unidentified white particulate matter (likely perlite or mineral salts).
biome_observations.fungal_presence: None detected, though the lack of plant life suggests a potential collapse of the rhizosphere.

temporal_deltas.T_earliest_to_T_minus_4: Minimal change; pots appear devoid of foliage.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_4_to_T_minus_3: Introduction of a metal spoon into the yellow pot (P1).
temporal_deltas.T_minus_3_to_T_minus_2: Removal of the spoon; continued absence of plant matter.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_2_to_T_minus_1: Introduction of a white cylindrical object near the pots.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_1_to_current: Stabilization of the empty state; no recovery or new growth.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable. There is no photosynthetic activity. The 'Systemic Loss' status is confirmed across all registry items.
anomalies: Total absence of the scale anchor (White Rabbit) in the current frame.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a complete failure of the botanical biome. Over the 5-day observation period, no plant life was observed in any of the pots. The environment has been used for the temporary placement of non-botanical objects, suggesting the pots are currently being repurposed or are in a state of total neglect. The soil appears dark and potentially waterlogged, but without biological occupants, it is effectively inert.
